dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 171 for suspected pre-term labor at a residence in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Lee, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I'm having contractions. They came on stronger today, especially after I came home from the store.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes for me?
Yeah, I can.
Can you tell me where we are and what you're thinking right now?
I'm at home. I'm having contractions and I just want it to end. I'm in a lot of pain.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: You mentioned the contractions started after you came home from the store. Can you tell me when the pain started earlier today and what it was like?
I had some abdominal pain and nausea this morning. Now it's much worse, and I've been throwing up too.
You mentioned this is a high-risk pregnancy. Can you tell us about your past pregnancies?
I'm G6, P4, A2. My current pregnancy is considered high-risk.
Ma'am, I'm going to take your blood pressure now.
Your blood pressure is 134 over 103, which is elevated.
And your breathing is 24 times a minute, fast and regular.
Your heart rate is 126. It's elevated but regular.
Your oxygen level is 96 percent on room air.
Your blood sugar is 104. I need you to keep your arm steady for a moment, please.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: You're breathing pretty quickly and deeply. Is it hard for you to take a full breath during the contractions?
I'm going to listen to your lungs now. Can you take slow, deep breaths?
Your airway is patent and you are maintaining it well.
Your skin looks normal and you appear warm. I'll keep an eye on that.
I feel a strong radial pulse on your wrist and it has steady strength.
Your skin is pink, not jaundiced or pale. Any bruises or rashes you'd like to mention?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: I'm going to ask you four quick questions to check your awareness. First, can you tell me your name?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: I'm Christie.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: Where are we right now?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: We are in my bedroom.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: What is today's date?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: October 29th.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: What led you to call 911 today?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: Because I'm having contractions and they are getting stronger. My mother called from my phone when I didn't answer it.
I want to confirm-when the contractions occur, does it become harder for you to breathe?
Any vaginal bleeding or spotting today?
Your skin is warm and dry, which is good.

dialogue.micro_intent: genitourinary exam - rupture of the amniotic sac
dialogue.utterance: Have you noticed any leaking of your amniotic fluid?
You seem to be thinking clearly. Any confusion or changes in how you feel mentally?
I need to start a cardiac monitor, which will check your heart rhythm and rate more closely. Can I go ahead with that?
Yeah, that's fine. I trust you.
Monitor is on. The rhythm looks like sinus tachycardia with no other abnormalities.
I'm going to start an IV in your arm now so we can give fluids and possibly medication if needed. Is that okay?
IV started in left antecubital vein with first attempt. Fluids are flowing well.
ECG results are sinus tachycardia with no other abnormalities.
I just rechecked your blood sugar and it's not registering. We'll check it again soon but our initial reading was 104.
We're activating the OB/GYN protocol for pre-term labor as per hospital standing orders. We'll transport you to [COMPANY_NAME].
Breathing is 20 per minute. No abnormalities detected.
Pulse is 114. Still elevated, but regular.
Blood pressure is now 125 over 84, which is still high.
SpO2 is 98 percent, which is an improvement.
We still can't read the blood sugar; initial was 104 and this one shows 0. We'll note the unreadable reading but no cause for alarm today.
Are the contractions getting more intense or is there a specific number you'd use to describe the pain?
A 10. If I could describe your typical headache as a 5, these are 10s every minute or so.
I want to confirm your mental status is normal. First, can you tell me your name?
Christie.
What is the name of this city we're in?
[CITY].
What is the current date?
October 29th.
There is some tenderness but no rigidity or guarding noted on the abdominal exam.
Skin turgor looks good. We're not seeing any signs of dehydration or abnormal elasticity.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Your pupils are equal and reactive to light, which is good.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck veins
dialogue.utterance: I'm not seeing any distended jugular veins in your neck.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck masses
dialogue.utterance: No masses or lymphadenopathy in the neck area.

dialogue.micro_intent: respiratory status - Position of the trachea
dialogue.utterance: Your trachea is in the midline.
I'm not seeing any uneven chest motion during your breathing. It's symmetrical.

dialogue.micro_intent: respiratory status - Peripheral vs. Central Pulse Comparison
dialogue.utterance: Your pulses are comparable in strength from your wrist to your neck.
I just wanted to confirm-your lungs are loud and clear from side to side. No new sounds.
We don't have a record of any urinary changes, but we're monitoring you closely.
Blood pressure is elevated, but there are no signs of preeclampsia or eclampsia at this time.
I don't hear any rales, wheezes, or crackles in your lungs.
I'm not pressing on your pelvis, but you can tell me if there are any new areas of pain down below.
We will be going to [COMPANY_NAME], is that okay with you?
Christie, we'll be rolling you out to the ambulance now and placing you on the stretcher. Foster, be ready to assist.
Christie, this next part may be uncomfortable, but we're getting you to the hospital in a safe and stable condition. We'll see you through this together.
We are heading to [COMPANY_NAME] cold with OB/gyn pre-term labor protocol activation.
We have her condition stabilized with IV access, vital signs documented, and ECG setup. G6, P4, A2 with high-risk history. Contractions two minutes apart. En route report is complete.
Consent and billing signatures secured. Patient has been transported to [COMPANY_NAME] and is up to the 6th floor with care turned over to the RN on duty.
